# Christopher Mercer

> Systems architect designing institutional-grade blockchain infrastructure across multiple protocols. Multi-client protocol engineering, smart contracts, tokenization, governance and custody architecture, formal verification.

## About

Christopher Mercer is a systems architect who designs institutional-grade blockchain infrastructure across multiple protocol families and client implementations. He works across the full vertical - from consensus-layer protocol design and cryptographic security through smart contracts, tokenization workflows, governance systems, and the interfaces that sit on top. Traditional finance background applied to blockchain systems engineering.

## What He Designs & Builds

### Protocol Engineering
Designs and implements consensus-layer upgrades across multiple blockchain clients written in different languages (Go, JVM/Scala, Java). Integrates execution-layer proposals spanning dynamic fee markets, account abstraction, and transient storage. Coordinates cross-client interoperability testing on live testnets. Operates full nodes across multiple client implementations.

### Governance & Custody Systems
Architects governance and custody infrastructure for institutional blockchain environments - from non-transferable credential-based voting to DAO frameworks to prediction-market-based decision mechanisms. On-chain treasury systems with custody isolation, separation-of-duties enforcement, and auditable execution pipelines. Designs on-chain identity registries, decentralized exchange protocol architecture, and token launch platforms. Rigorous formal verification - fuzz testing, invariant checks, security proofs.

### Smart Contracts & Tokenization
Designs and deploys smart contract systems spanning token standards (ERC-20, ERC-721, ERC-1400), tokenization workflows, and DeFi primitives including staking, liquidity provisioning, and lending protocols. Full-stack delivery from Solidity to production interface.

### Data Architecture & Infrastructure
Designs pipelines processing over a billion records. Performance-engineered storage with order-of-magnitude throughput improvements. Multi-pass enrichment architectures. Full-text search infrastructure across millions of documents.

## How He Works

Phase-based delivery with formal handoff documentation. Cross-functional coordination across multi-client, multi-language environments. Security-first methodology: adversarial modeling, formal verification, and fuzz testing as standard practice. Traditional finance discipline applied to blockchain engineering - custody isolation, separation of duties, auditability.

## Focus Areas

- Protocol Engineering
- Smart Contracts & Tokenization
- Governance & Custody Architecture
- Multi-Client Infrastructure
- Security & Formal Verification
- Data Systems
